ticketTitle: Update report consumers to skip Unsupported records in aggregate calculations

nonGoals: Do not add serialization/JSON custom handling
deliverables.objective: Add Unsupported bool field to domain.CertificationRecord struct with default false for backward compatibility
deliverables.planText: Add `Unsupported bool` field to the CertificationRecord struct in internal/domain/record.go. Place it near the existing bool fields (e.g. after `ReportCardExcluded`). Use a json tag with omitempty: `json:"unsupported,omitempty"`. Add a brief doc comment explaining that unsupported records are excluded from aggregate calculations. Default false ensures all existing code and tests continue to work unchanged. Run `go test ./... -count=1` to confirm nothing breaks.

nonGoals: Do not modify other report consumers (card, detailed, full)
Do not change status/grade counting logic for supported records
deliverables.objective: Skip Unsupported records in Health() aggregate with corrected denominator and zero-guard
deliverables.planText: In internal/report/health.go Health(): add `if r.Unsupported { continue }` at the top of the records loop. Track a `counted` int for non-unsupported records. Use `counted` instead of `len(records)` for TotalUnits, PassRate denominator, and AverageScore denominator. After the loop, add a guard: if `counted == 0`, return an empty HealthReport{} to prevent division-by-zero when all records are unsupported. In internal/report/health_test.go: add a table-driven subtest that passes records with Unsupported:true mixed with supported records, asserting that the unsupported ones contribute zero to counts, scores, and pass-rate. Also add a subtest where all records are unsupported and assert an empty HealthReport is returned.

nonGoals: Do not modify other report consumers (health, detailed, full)
Do not change GradeDistribution or TopIssues logic for supported records except to exclude unsupported
deliverables.objective: Skip Unsupported records in GenerateCard() aggregates with corrected TotalUnits and zero-guard
deliverables.planText: In internal/report/card.go GenerateCard(): add `if r.Unsupported { continue }` at the top of the records loop. Track a `counted` int. Use `counted` for TotalUnits, OverallScore denominator, and PassRate denominator. After the loop, if `counted == 0`, return the N/A card (OverallGrade="N/A") to match existing empty-record behavior. In buildTopIssues(), add the same `if r.Unsupported { continue }` guard so unsupported records do not appear in top issues. In internal/report/card_test.go: add subtests with Unsupported:true records mixed with supported records, verifying OverallScore, PassRate, GradeDistribution, and TotalUnits exclude unsupported records. Add a subtest where all records are unsupported and assert the N/A card is returned.

nonGoals: Do not modify findExpiringSoon, findHighestRisk, findFailing, or findRecurrentlyFailing functions
Do not modify other report consumers
Do not change dimension/language aggregation math for supported records
deliverables.objective: Skip Unsupported records in computeDimensionAverages and computeLanguageBreakdowns in detailed.go
deliverables.planText: In internal/report/detailed.go computeDimensionAverages(): add `if r.Unsupported { continue }` at the top of the loop so unsupported records contribute nothing to dimension score sums or counts. In computeLanguageBreakdowns(): add the same guard at the top of the loop so unsupported records are excluded from per-language totals, score sums, and passing counts. The existing `counts[dim] > 0` and `total > 0` guards already handle the all-unsupported-for-a-language case correctly. In internal/report/detailed_test.go: add subtests that insert Unsupported:true records and assert they contribute zero to dimension averages and language breakdown totals, scores, and passing tallies.

nonGoals: Do not modify other functions in full.go
Do not modify other report consumers
Do not change per-language score/grade/passing aggregation logic for supported records
deliverables.objective: Skip Unsupported records in buildLanguageDetail in full.go
deliverables.planText: In internal/report/full.go buildLanguageDetail(): add `if r.Unsupported { continue }` at the top of the records loop. This prevents unsupported records from being added to per-language score slices, grade distribution maps, and passing counts. If all records for a language are unsupported, that language simply won't appear in the langAccum map, which is the correct behavior. In internal/report/full_test.go: add subtests with Unsupported:true records verifying they do not affect per-language score averages, grade distributions, or passing counts in the FullReport output.
